artifacts.content:
Hi Elena,

Lucy finally crashed, so I’m stealing ten quiet minutes with the laptop. I was looking at listings again in Multnomah Village and Hillsdale, and it keeps circling back to the same thing: we really do need to stay within that 15–20 minute drive of Doernbecher. After the asthma ER runs in March 2024 and that earlier febrile seizure in March 2023, I keep hearing Dr. Patel in my head telling us not to drift too far if we can help it.

On top of that, your PICU nights and 24‑hour call blocks since January 2023 are killing your sleep as it is. With all the traffic noise on SW Vermont and the bar on the corner going until 1 a.m., it’s no wonder Megan brought up fatigue again in your June 2024 review. We really need a super‑quiet primary bedroom on the calm side of the house, plus decent insulation, so you can actually get 6–7 hours of daytime sleep.

I’m also thinking about Lucy’s allergies and asthma. After the anaphylaxis at Randall Children’s in March 2023 and Dr. Patel’s October 2023 warning about the damp basement here on Vermont probably feeding her nighttime wheezing, anything with prior water intrusion or mold is an automatic no. We’ll need space in her room for a HEPA setup.

So: quiet street, newer construction or at least a dry basement, and still close to OHSU/Doernbecher by car or bus. That probably keeps us in Hillsdale/Multnomah instead of way out in Aloha.

When you get a second between charts tomorrow, can you sanity‑check this short list I starred? If we can agree on the “musts” this week, I’ll email an agent and see about Saturday showings.

Love,
Daniel

Hey Miguel,

Just got off another early Zoom with the San Jose devs from the back patio, and I swear the whole block at 4132 SW Vermont could hear me ranting about the fare‑evasion pilot and that written warning Mark Ellison gave me last November for criticizing it on Slack. It really drives home how tiny this yard is and how close the neighbors are.

When TriMet shifted us to the hybrid schedule in January 2024, I thought it’d be nice doing 7 a.m. calls outside, but with the postage‑stamp lot and both neighbors basically on top of us, there’s zero privacy. Between that and the domestic violence arrest next door after the 2 a.m. party last May 2023, I’m done with party‑ish blocks and paper‑thin buffers.

So for the new place I’m pushing for something closer to a 5,000–6,000 sq ft lot, maybe even the full 6,000, so I can take calls without broadcasting TriMet drama to half of Southwest Portland. Dr. Liu at Portland Anxiety Clinic also keeps reminding me to protect my stress levels after the Ross Island Bridge panic attack in March 2023, so a bigger, quieter yard where I can pace during calls without staring at traffic sounds pretty ideal.

We’re still trying to stay within 20 minutes of Doernbecher for Lucy’s quarterly neuro follow‑ups after the June 2024 seizure, so that limits us to the inner west side. But there are a couple of Multnomah Village places with 5,500–6,000 sq ft lots and decent bike routes to Center Street that look promising.

How’s life down there? Still thinking about coming up for a long weekend this fall? We could drag Lucy and you out to Tryon Creek again.

– D

metadata.subject: Why I suddenly care about yard size (and quiet)

artifacts.content:
Apple Note – House‑hunting brain dump

Non‑negotiables (as of 2024‑07‑10):

• Suburbs: Inner west side only – Hillsdale, Multnomah Village, maybe Raleigh Hills or Beaverton near Beaverton Creek MAX/Elmonica if commute works. Need to stay within ~15–20 min of OHSU Doernbecher for Lucy’s asthma + seizure stuff and her quarterly neuro follow‑ups after June 2024.

• Commute/mental health: Per Dr. Liu (Portland Anxiety Clinic, March 2023 Ross Island Bridge panic attack), must be able to bike to TriMet Center Street garage in under 30 minutes. Avoid daily driving over Ross Island or highways.

• Property type: Detached single‑family only. No more duplexes after the downstairs neighbor at 4132 SW Vermont called the police twice in March 2024 over Lucy’s tantrums and my late TriMet Zooms. Also never again with HOAs after the SE 52nd townhouse hit us with that $300 fine in May 2022 for Hye‑Jin’s catering prep.

• Lot size: Target 4,000–6,000 sq ft. Enough for fenced yard for future medium‑sized rescue dog (Oregon Humane Society application submitted Feb 2, 2024), veggie beds, and maybe a flat walking loop for Jin‑Soo per Dr. Patel’s March 2024 cardiology advice after his near‑miss on SW Vermont in Feb.

• Layout/family: Ground‑floor bedroom + full bath if possible for Mamá’s Parkinson’s/neuropathy (Kaiser San Jose Feb 2024 + Dec 2024 hospital stay). Need space for 2–4 month visits starting summer/fall 2025.

• Safety/quiet: Away from bars/cannabis shops and big party apartment complexes after the April 2023 break‑in and May 2023 DV arrest next door.

Review this list with Elena before talking seriously to any agent.

Mami,

I wanted to update you on why we’re being so picky about houses. It’s not that we’re trying to be fancy; it’s just everything that’s happened the last couple of years.

You remember October 2022, when the upstairs neighbor’s Lab mix bit Lucy in the little shared courtyard behind 4132 SW Vermont? After that we promised you we’d only move if we could get our own fenced yard, at least ~4,500 sq ft, so she’s not sharing space with random dogs. Then there was the attempted break‑in on our block in October 2023, when the Portland Police officer told us Vermont is on their burglary “hot spot” list. Lucy still wakes up screaming when she hears sirens.

On top of that, her asthma and allergies have been rough. She had the peanut anaphylaxis at Randall Children’s in March 2023, and then multiple ER visits at Doernbecher in March 2024 for wheezing. Dr. Patel at OHSU said in October 2023 that the damp basement here is probably making her nighttime breathing worse. So we’re avoiding any house with a history of water intrusion or mold and making sure there’s room in her bedroom for a HEPA filter.

We’re also thinking about you. With the Parkinson’s diagnosis at Kaiser San Jose in February 2024 and your neuropathy getting worse after the December 2024 hospitalization, we want a place where you can stay 2–4 months at a time starting next year, with a ground‑floor bedroom and bathroom so you don’t have to do stairs.

It sounds like a long list, but it’s really just: safe street, dry house, decent yard, and space for you and Papá. We’ll send you photos once we find something that feels right.

Te quiero,
Daniel

metadata.subject: Por qué somos tan exigentes con la casa

artifacts.content:
Event: Lucy – Neuro follow‑up + errands

Date: 2024-09-19
Time: 1:30–4:30 p.m.
Location: OHSU Doernbecher Children’s Hospital – Pediatric Neurology Clinic, Portland
Attendees: Daniel, Elena, Lucia

Notes:
• Quarterly neuro follow‑up after June 2024 seizure episode. Confirm meds, ask about any new red flags for nighttime seizures and how close we really need to stay to Doernbecher (goal = within 15–20 minute drive from home).
• Bring list of March 2024 asthma ER visits, plus Dr. Patel’s October 2023 allergist notes about damp basement at 4132 SW Vermont contributing to nighttime wheezing.
• Ask whether HEPA filter + avoiding mold/water‑damage listings is enough, or if we should also avoid houses closer to wildfire smoke corridors.
• After appointment, quick stop at Portland Anxiety Clinic so I can drop off updated insurance info for Dr. Liu (Ross Island Bridge panic attack follow‑up from March 2023; she wanted an update on how bike commuting to TriMet Center Street is going).
• If Lucy’s doing okay, swing by Hillsdale Library and drive a couple of streets in Multnomah Village and Raleigh Hills to get a feel for which blocks seem quieter/less bar‑heavy than our current SW Vermont spot (esp. after April 2023 break‑in + bar‑related suspects).
• Remember to text Miguel a quick summary for Mamá so she doesn’t worry.

metadata.title: Lucy – Doernbecher neuro follow-up + housing scouting

artifacts.content:
Daniel: You still up for dinner at your parents’ in Beaverton tonight?

Elena: Yeah, but I might need to crash on their couch for a bit. Last night’s PICU shift was brutal.

Daniel: I figured. Between the 24‑hr call blocks since Jan 2023 and the traffic noise on SW Vermont, no wonder Megan flagged your fatigue in June. Another reason to get you a quieter bedroom.

Elena: Seriously. And I still tense up thinking about those MAX rides last winter (Jan–Feb 2024). Dr. Han was right – daily rail commute is not my friend.

Daniel: Which is why Multnomah Village/Hillsdale > deep Beaverton, even though Elmonica/Beaverton Creek would make TriMet happy if that June 2024 PIP talk with Kelly turns into a 7 a.m. on‑site requirement at Center Street 3 days a week.

Elena: Ugh, that PIP thing still bugs me. But yeah, my panic attacks on MAX + Lucy’s stuff mean closer in is better.

Daniel: Speaking of Lucy, she woke up from another nightmare about the April 2023 break‑in and the May 2023 DV arrest next door. Dr. Nguyen at Portland Family Counseling keeps saying moving away from bars/party blocks will help.

Elena: Add that to the list with the October 2023 “burglary hot spot” comment from the cop. I want her preschool years somewhere quieter.

Daniel: Same. Let’s talk to your dad tonight about yards big enough for his walking laps (Dr. Patel’s March 2024 cardiology advice) and maybe a shed for your mom’s catering stuff.

Elena: Deal. Leaving Doernbecher now, see you in 20.

Allison,

Following up on our earlier conversations with Risk after the November 2022 collision at Barbur/Terwilliger. I’m in the middle of house‑hunting, and I want to make sure my future commute setup aligns with what you and Risk recommended.

You’d suggested back then that, if I keep commuting by e‑bike to HQ/Center Street, I should avoid leaving the bike outside overnight because of theft and liability concerns. So one of my non‑negotiables now is a garage or secure shed where I can lock up the e‑bike and helmet. Our current place at 4132 SW Vermont barely has room for Lucy’s scooter, much less safe storage.

I’m also trying to follow Dr. Liu’s guidance from Portland Anxiety Clinic after the Ross Island Bridge panic attack in March 2023. She wants me to minimize car trips over the bridge and stick to a bikeable radius to the TriMet Center Street garage (ideally under 30 minutes). That’s pushing us toward inner west‑side neighborhoods like Hillsdale or Multnomah Village rather than farther‑flung suburbs.

On the work side, I’m assuming the June 2024 performance improvement plan discussion with Kelly might still result in a more formal expectation that I be on site at Center Street by 7:00 a.m. three days a week. I’m factoring that into our search as well, looking at places near Elmonica/SW 170th or Beaverton Creek MAX as a backup if we can’t find something closer in that checks the safety boxes for my family.

If there are any updated guidelines from Risk about staff biking to late‑night incident calls (e.g., after serious events like the April 2022 Rose Quarter collision), I’d appreciate it so I can keep those in mind while we decide.

Best,
Daniel
